top5Opportunities.statement: Minimize keybar geometry drift under 2x line-frequency electromagnetic forcing (100-120 Hz) causing progressive stator slot deformation >0.05 mm over service life
Minimize the likelihood of keybar slot-contact surface deviation >+-0.05 mm causing lamination misalignment during stator core stacking
Minimize stator core structural failures from keybar fatigue cracking under cyclic electromagnetic forcing over a 30-year machine operating lifetime
Minimize dimensional deviation of installed keybars from the stator design envelope exceeding 0.03 mm on lamination-contact surfaces after final core assembly
Increase the ability to source qualified keybar profiles for any required cross-section geometry within 4 weeks to avoid stator assembly schedule delays

needs.statement: Minimize keybar geometry drift under 2x line-frequency electromagnetic forcing (100-120 Hz) causing progressive stator slot deformation >0.05 mm over the machine's service life
needs.importanceRationale: In-service fatigue and EM forcing resistance is the #1 long-term reliability concern for stator structures. Cold-drawn grain flow alignment improves fatigue endurance; 42CrMo4 Q&T shows no HCF failure between 10^6 and 10^9 cycles at UTS <1,400 MPa.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of keybar slot-contact surface deviation >+-0.05 mm causing lamination misalignment during stator core stacking
needs.importanceRationale: IT8 cold-drawn tolerance (+-0.02 mm) directly addresses this need. CNC-machined incumbents at +-0.05-0.1 mm are underperforming.

needs.statement: Minimize stator core structural failures from keybar fatigue cracking under cyclic electromagnetic forcing over a 30-year machine operating lifetime
needs.importanceRationale: 30-year lifetime fatigue integrity is mission-critical. Grain-flow-aligned cold-drawn 42CrMo4: fatigue limit ~480 MPa at 10^7 cycles.

needs.statement: Minimize dimensional deviation of installed keybars from the stator design envelope exceeding 0.03 mm on lamination-contact surfaces after final core assembly
needs.importanceRationale: IT8 = +-0.02 mm directly achieves 0.03 mm contact-face target with margin. CNC job-shop typical +-0.05 mm.

needs.statement: Increase the ability to source qualified keybar profiles for any required cross-section geometry within 4 weeks to avoid stator assembly schedule delays
needs.importanceRationale: Repeat dies can be delivered in 2-3 weeks; new geometry requires 6-12 weeks. CNC in-house is queue-dependent.
